The SLAY.

May 2026

Word of the Month: Demonstrate

Word of the Month

Word of the Month

Demonstrate

What students are now able to show

This past week, Amy, Lailanie, and I stood in Sofia's classroom at Dimmitt Middle School and listened to students share spoken word poems built from the real migration stories of community members — people who had been interviewed beforehand, who sat in the audience that day, hearing their own journeys reflected back through the voices of young people.

"I carried my mother's feelings in suitcases."

"My compass spun spun spun to a different world under a different sun."

"Loving a home is also learning when you must let go."

These weren't just poems. They were demonstrations — of historical understanding, of empathy, of what becomes possible when students are given structure, purpose, and a real audience.

To demonstrate is to make learning visible — in what students produce, and in what teachers have made possible. State assessments ask students to show what they know. End of Year conversations ask corps members to show what's changed. What has taken root — however it looks — is about to show itself.

That's true for your students. It's also true for you. What you've built this year is real. Trust what the evidence shows.

As you move through May, consider

What are students showing now that wasn't visible in September?
Where did students shape the learning, not just receive it?
What are your students showing you about where they are?
What have your students shown you about yourself this year?

— DR.

Required Action Items

EOY Checklist ✅

📋

The final stretch — make it count.

Seven required tasks between now and your last day of school. Click each item to track your progress as you complete them.

Your progress0 of 7 done
End-of-Year Survey
Due Fri 5/29

The TFA End-of-Year survey gives you the opportunity to share reflections from your experience this school year so that we can strengthen and improve CM support for the next school year. Thank you in advance for sharing your perspective!

Don't forget to use your TFA Google account! If you have any issues accessing it, please reach out to your MDLD or MD, Ops.

TFA OneApp
AmeriCorps: EOY Performance Review
Due May 22

Arrives via DocuSign in your TFA inbox. Access Code: TEACH2026. Your coach completes first, then routes to you for signature.

AmeriCorps: Exit Form
Due May 22

Complete in the myAmeriCorps portal. Reset login via hotline 1-800-942-2677 if needed.

Due May 22

Your principal receives a DocuSign from Caitlin Tolson. Follow up the week of May 11 to avoid delays to your Education Award.

Community & Connection

Connect with a Local Alum

Jenny Rodriguez (Gates Foundation, K–12 Ed) is eager to connect with 2nd-year CMs thinking about what's next. She taught 10 years, worked TFA's corps and alumni teams, and is back in Seattle.

Book in-person →
Book virtual →

Weekend Office Hours

Need a quiet, caffeinated workspace on a weekend? Office Captains have you covered with work space, community, and supplies — anchor chart paper, markers, stickers for grading.

View Calendar →

🤝 Support an Incoming Corps Member

Want to support an incoming corps member by chatting with them all things hiring and moving? Let Amy, Damian, Lailanie, or Tierra know!

Cool Community Organization

You're Invited!! 🎉

Calling all 1st- and 2nd-year Teach For America corps members! Local Partner Organization, Junior Achievement, is inviting you to their Summer Camp to explore hands-on, real-world learning experiences designed to engage and inspire students.

📅 Thursday, July 30  |  9:00 AM – 2:00 PM

Drop in to check out BizTown and Finance Park, or jump in as a volunteer!

  • BizTown is an immersive learning experience where students run a simulated town—taking on jobs, earning paychecks, running businesses, and making civic decisions.
  • Finance Park helps students explore personal finance by managing a real-world budget and making decisions about housing, transportation, savings, and more.

📅 Friday, July 31  |  9:00 AM – 2:00 PM

Come watch our students showcase their ideas during Shark Tank–style presentations, where they pitch business concepts and practice public speaking, critical thinking, and financial literacy.

Both days will also include tours of our space, opportunities to review open curriculum, and time to sign up or learn more about bringing these programs to your students.

Whether you want to volunteer, observe, or simply learn what BizTown and Finance Park are all about, we'd love to have you stop by!

Resource Corner

Spring Strategies & Wellness

BetterLesson: EOY Strategies

The end of the school year is this beautiful time where in many cases you are able to try things and do things that you either were not able to get to during the year or that would not neatly fit into the very set schedule that you had to move through for your curriculum. Check out these resources as you determine how to engage your students and interest them in topics, learning, and activities that they may not get otherwise.

  • StrategyFinal Project — this project has multiple components, make sure to check out each one
  • StrategyReflecting on Learning
  • StrategyService Learning Project
  • StrategySenior Project Presentations

Free Therapy — BetterHelp

Individual therapy at no cost through May 31, 2026. Up to 3 sessions/month. Taking care of yourself is part of the work — not optional.

CM Getting Started Guide →

Commemorative Months

May Observances

🌟 Teacher Appreciation Week (May 5–9)

Celebrating You

May is Teacher Appreciation Month — and this week is for you. Your commitment to students, families, and communities doesn't go unnoticed. Thank you for showing up every day with intention, care, and courage.

Have a milestone, win, or classroom moment worth celebrating? Share it with Lailanie and we'll feature it in the next Slay!

🌺 Asian American & Pacific Islander Heritage Month

AAPI Heritage Month Resources

May is AAPI Heritage Month — a time to celebrate the histories, contributions, and cultures of Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders. Bring these stories into your classroom and school community.

  • ClassroomTeaching AAPI History — Learning for Justice
  • ClassroomPBS Educator Resources for AAPI Month
  • ReadingNEA: AAPI Heritage Month

✊ Mental Health Awareness Month

Mental Health in the Classroom & Beyond

May is Mental Health Awareness Month. Supporting student wellbeing is inseparable from supporting academic growth — and that starts with taking care of your own mental health too.

  • For YouBetterHelp — Free sessions through May 31
  • ClassroomNAMI: Ending the Silence (classroom program)
  • ReadingEdutopia: Trauma-Sensitive Teaching

Have a resource, story, or observance to add for this month or next? Let Lailanie know!

Send to Lailanie →

CM Celebrations & Shout Outs

Recognize & Celebrate

🌸
May Birthdays
April showers bring May flowers — and these May birthdays are springing up to celebrate! 🌷

Faith Richardson

May 26

May 31
Miriam Stolle
Sofia

Students created powerful spoken word poems and presented them to real audiences. She's building a space where student voices are genuinely heard.

Juan

Building a classroom grounded in strong relationships and clear structure — pushing himself to make sure every student truly feels that care, not just sees it.

Nik

Creating genuine belonging. His focus on meaningful work and productive struggle is helping students think more deeply and build lasting understanding.

Tu

The trusting relationships he's built are helping students understand what they're working toward — and take real ownership of getting there.

Mai Li

Sustained growth across multiple areas. Her focus on supportive teaching and planning for misconceptions is opening doors for more students to access learning.

Miriam

One of the highest Cultivate perception scores in the cohort. The way she centers student identity isn't just in the data — her students feel it every day.

AJ

Building a classroom where student voice is elevated and identities are affirmed. His students feel seen and heard — and his focus on feedback will take them further.

Joe

Shout out to Joe for stepping into a new chapter — moving from a co-teaching role into leading his own classroom next year. The way he has built strong relationships, stayed grounded in structure, and taken ownership even when sharing a classroom has set him up for this moment. Excited to see him carry that same presence and impact into a space that's fully his.

Ash

Shout out to Ash for leveraging creativity to engage students in learning, breathing new life into lessons, and reaching for higher levels of rigor!

Jared

Shout out to Jared! When prepping students for state testing, Jared has managed to build excitement, boost confidence, and ease fears!

Linda B.

Shout out to Linda B. for sharing heritage week resources. It's inspiring to learn about what you've done and we're grateful you're passing your knowledge to others.

Natalie

Shout out to Natalie for an ongoing commitment to learning and trying new things. It's not always easy to do and you are an awesome model for us all!

Sam

Shout out to Sam for stepping into a new role next year! After leveraging every opportunity to exercise those leadership skills this year, Sam will be leading a 5th grade classroom next year!

Have a shout out? Send it to Amy or Damian.

Sincerely, Lailanie

⚾ Baseball Season is in Full Swing!

We're working on getting FREE regular level 300 tickets like last year — it was a ball! You can also catch the Mariners for fairly cheap at most games, but if you want to splurge a little, grab promotional tickets: special jerseys, heritage celebrations, fun souvenirs — the full experience.

☕ Newest Café Find — Moment Coffee

If you're looking for a new spot to work, catch up, or just treat yourself, check out Moment Coffee in Fremont. It's cozy, the vibe is right, and it's giving everything a neighborhood café should. See for yourself:
